questions.q: You want to create a new deployment named `web-app` using the `nginx` image with 3 replicas, but instead of applying it immediately, you want to save the YAML definition to a file named `deploy.yaml`. Which command does this?
options: kubectl create deployment web-app --image=nginx --replicas=3 --dry-run=client -o yaml > deploy.yaml
kubectl create deployment web-app --image=nginx --replicas=3 -o yaml > deploy.yaml
questions.explanation: The `--dry-run=client` flag tells kubectl to process the command without sending it to the API server. Combined with `-o yaml`, it prints the generated YAML manifest to standard output, which you can redirect (`>`) to a file.

questions.q: A pod named `api-pod` is listening on port 8080 inside the cluster. You want to test it locally from your workstation's port 9000. Which command accomplishes this?
questions.explanation: The `kubectl port-forward` command uses the format `local-port:remote-port`. Therefore, `9000:8080` forwards traffic from port 9000 on your local machine to port 8080 on the pod.

questions.q: Which output format should you use with `kubectl` to see additional columns for Pods, such as the Node they are running on and their IP address?
questions.explanation: The `-o wide` flag instructs kubectl to display additional, commonly requested columns that are normally hidden to save horizontal space, such as the pod IP and the node it is scheduled on.
